1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: Antenatal care is best defined as:
options: A preventive obstetric program from conception to onset of labor
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Antenatal care is a preventive obstetric program including observations, medical care, investigations and advice from conception to labor onset.

1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.q: All of the following are objectives of antenatal care EXCEPT:
options: Giving advice for all pregnant patients
1-definition-and-objectives-of-antenatal.explain: Objectives include identifying/monitoring high-risk pregnancy, early detection of complications, advice, and managing minor problems. Labor management is intrapartum care.

2-frequency-of-visits.q: For low-risk pregnancies, follow-up visits are scheduled:
options: Monthly till 28 weeks, every 2 weeks till 36 weeks, then weekly
Only at booking and at 36 weeks
2-frequency-of-visits.explain: Low-risk schedule: every month till 28 weeks, every 2 weeks till 36 weeks, then every week till delivery.

2-frequency-of-visits.q: For high-risk pregnancies after 36 weeks, the recommendation is:
2-frequency-of-visits.explain: High-risk schedule: every 2 weeks till 28 weeks, every week till 36 weeks, then hospitalization till delivery.

3-initial-visit.q: The initial antenatal visit includes all EXCEPT:
options: Diagnosis of pregnancy and accurate dating
3-initial-visit.explain: The initial visit includes pregnancy diagnosis, dating, and complete clinical evaluation (history, general, abdominal, and local examination).

4-subsequent-visits.q: Which of the following is routinely checked at each subsequent antenatal visit?
options: BP, weight, edema, and abdominal examination
4-subsequent-visits.explain: Each visit includes BP, weight, edema check, and abdominal examination. Urine for albumin/sugar is also checked.

5-screening-tests.q: Routine antenatal screening tests include all EXCEPT:
5-screening-tests.explain: Routine tests: ABO/Rh typing, CBC, postprandial glucose, HBsAg, rubella antibodies, cervicovaginal smear, urine analysis. Amniocentesis is a diagnostic test done only when indicated.

6-antenatal-advice-nutrition.q: The daily iron requirement during pregnancy is:
6-antenatal-advice-nutrition.explain: Daily iron requirement is 30–60 mg. Calcium: 1.5 g, folic acid: 400 µg, total calories: 2700 cal/day.

6-antenatal-advice-nutrition.q: Daily folic acid requirement during pregnancy is:
6-antenatal-advice-nutrition.explain: Folic acid requirement is 400 µg/day. Total weight gain during pregnancy should be 10–12 kg.

7-antenatal-advice-lifestyle.q: Antenatal lifestyle advice includes all EXCEPT:
options: 8 hours sleep at night and 2 in afternoon
7-antenatal-advice-lifestyle.explain: Advice includes: light clothing, avoid hot baths and swimming, adequate sleep, and avoidance of smoking/alcohol. Heavy exercise is not recommended.

8-immunization-during-pregnancy.q: Which of the following vaccines is safe during pregnancy?
8-immunization-during-pregnancy.explain: Inactivated viral vaccines (influenza, rabies) are safe. Live attenuated vaccines (MMR, polio) are contraindicated.

9-exposure-to-infection.q: Which infection is particularly teratogenic during pregnancy?
9-exposure-to-infection.explain: Rubella and cytomegalovirus are teratogenic and should be avoided during pregnancy.

10-exposure-to-irradiation.q: A radiation dose of 50–100 rads during pregnancy is associated with:
10-exposure-to-irradiation.explain: <5 rads: may not harm. 5–50 rads: may harm. 50–100 rads: CFMF (CNS malformation). Note: 1 rad = 0.01 Gy.

11-medications.q: The key principle regarding medication use in pregnancy is:
options: Avoid all drugs in the first trimester and give smallest effective dose for shortest time
11-medications.explain: Drugs should not be taken without obstetrician advice, all drugs should be avoided in the 1st trimester, and the smallest effective dose for the shortest time should be used.

12-warning-symptoms-of-pregnancy.q: Which of the following is a warning symptom of pregnancy that requires urgent review?
12-warning-symptoms-of-pregnancy.explain: Persistent headache, blurring of vision, vomiting, and epigastric pain suggest PIH/pre-eclampsia. Other warning signs: vaginal bleeding, decreased fetal kicks, severe abdominal pain.

12-warning-symptoms-of-pregnancy.q: All of the following are warning symptoms EXCEPT:
options: Mild nausea in the first trimester
12-warning-symptoms-of-pregnancy.explain: Mild nausea in early pregnancy is a normal symptom, not a warning sign. Warning signs include facial swelling, vaginal bleeding, decreased fetal kicks, fever, dyspnea, severe abdominal pain.
